layers.title: Worker Discovery Command
layers.summary: The PR adds a new dscli status command that discovers local datasystem workers by scanning process command lines for the existing -worker_address= signal. It filters matches to datasystem_worker processes, prints a PID/address table, treats no matches as success, and best-effort reports the same inventory to a central collector.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,205 @@
+_WORKER_ADDRESS_ARG = "-worker_address="
+_WORKER_BIN_RE = re.compile(r"(?:^|/)datasystem_worker(?=\s|$)")
+_WORKER_ADDRESS_RE = re.compile(r"-worker_address=(\S+)")
+def parse_worker_lines(output):
+    workers = []
+    for line in output.strip().splitlines():
+        parts = line.split(" ", 1)
+        if len(parts) != 2:
+            continue
+        pid_str, cmdline = parts
+        if not pid_str.isdigit():
+            continue
+        if not _WORKER_BIN_RE.search(cmdline):
+            continue
+        match = _WORKER_ADDRESS_RE.search(cmdline)
+        if not match:
+            continue
+        workers.append((int(pid_str), match.group(1)))
+    return workers
+class Command(BaseCommand):
+    name = "status"
+    description = "list running yuanrong datasystem worker services"
+    def run(self, args):
+        workers = self.list_workers()
+        self.report_status(workers)

layers.title: CLI Subcommand Registration
layers.summary: After the command module exists, the dispatcher is extended so dscli discovers and exposes it alongside start and stop. This is an additive CLI surface change with no removal or signature change to existing subcommands.

layers.title: Parser Payload Tests
layers.summary: The test layer validates the pure process-output parser and report payload builder without needing an installed package tree. Coverage includes empty output, multiple workers, numactl wrapping, IPv6 addresses, dscli decoys, malformed lines, and payload shape.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,146 @@
+class TestParseWorkerLines(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_multiple_workers(self):
+        output = (
+            "12345 /opt/ds/datasystem_worker --worker_address=127.0.0.1:31501\n"
+            "12346 /opt/ds/datasystem_worker --worker_address=127.0.0.1:31502 --log_dir=/var/log\n"
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            status.parse_worker_lines(output),
+            [(12345, "127.0.0.1:31501"), (12346, "127.0.0.1:31502")],
+        )
+class TestBuildReportPayload(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_payload_shape(self):
+        payload = status.build_report_payload(
+            [(12345, "127.0.0.1:31501"), (12346, "127.0.0.1:31502")],
+            "cluster-token-value",
+        )

layers.title: Design And Plan Docs
layers.summary: The final layer records the intended operator workflow, constraints, and implementation steps for the new status command. The docs describe the same pgrep-based discovery, stdout table, central collector reporting, and unit/manual validation strategy reflected in the code and tests.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,119 @@
+# `dscli status` Subcommand — Design Spec
+Add a read-only `dscli status` subcommand that lists the datasystem workers
+currently running on the local host, reporting each worker's **PID** and
+**worker address**, and publishes that inventory to the cluster's central
+**status collector** so operators get a fleet-wide view.
+Runs `pgrep -fa -- -worker_address=` with a 5s timeout.
+The report is authenticated with the **cluster access token**, read from the
+deployment's `.env` file (`DS_CLUSTER_TOKEN`), falling back to the
+`DS_CLUSTER_TOKEN` environment variable.

summary: This PR adds an operational dscli status subcommand that lists local worker PIDs and addresses, reports the inventory to a central collector, and documents/tests the new workflow.
